result:
Everything looks good. Here's a summary of what was implemented:

**Wolfram 1D Elementary Cellular Automaton Mode** ‚Äî toggled with `W` (uppercase):

**State variables** (in `__init__`):
- `wolfram_mode`, `wolfram_rule` (default: Rule 30), `wolfram_rows`, `wolfram_generation`
- `wolfram_notable` ‚Äî curated list of interesting rules: 30, 54, 60, 90, 110, 150, 182, 184, 250

**Controls** (`_handle_wolfram_input`):
- `Space` ‚Äî run/pause auto-advance
- `S` ‚Äî single step (compute one row)
- `F/G` or arrow keys ‚Äî cycle through notable rules
- `#` ‚Äî enter any rule number 0-255 via text prompt
- `R` ‚Äî randomize the initial row
- `C` ‚Äî reset to single center cell seed
- `+/-` ‚Äî speed control
- `W` or `Esc` ‚Äî exit back to normal mode
- `Q` ‚Äî quit

**Computation** (`_wolfram_tick`, `_wolfram_apply_rule`):
- Standard 3-cell neighborhood lookup against the 8-bit rule number
- Rows auto-scroll: only the most recent screen-height rows are kept

**Rendering** (`_draw_wolfram`):
- Cascading rows fill top-to-bottom with age-based color gradient (green‚Üícyan‚Üíyellow‚Üíred)
- Rule badge in top-right corner
- Visual rule diagram showing all 8 neighborhood‚Üíoutput mappings
- Status bar with rule number, binary representation, Wolfram class classification (I‚ÄìIV), row count, alive cells
- Help bar with all available shortcuts

**Other changes**:
- Main loop dispatches to Wolfram tick when in Wolfram mode
- Help bar in normal mode updated: `[W]olfram` added, save clarified as `[w]Save`
